title: olearia rani
intro: Olearia rani, also known as Rani or New Zealand Flax-leaved Daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in New Zealand for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations, including infusions and salves, that offer potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and promoting skin health. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's properties and confirm its effectiveness as a medicinal remedy.
